title: Spectrophotometric determination of nickel at microgram level in environmental matrices using 4-hydroxybenzaldehyde thiosemicarbazone
author: K. P. Satheesh
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Gates Institute of Technology, NH-44, Gootyanantapuramvillage, Gooty, 

 Anantapuram(District)-515 401, Andhra Pradesh, India 

 Department of Chemistry, Sri Krishnadevaraya University, Anantapur-515 003, Andhra Pradesh, India 

  Manuscript received online 24 September 2013, revised 01 November 2013, accepted 18 November 2013  

 The reagent 4-hydroxybenzaldehyde thiosemicarbazone (4-HBTS) has been synthesized, characterised and its analytical applications were investigated. The reagent 4-HBTS reacts with Ni II  in aqueous solution in the pH range 5–6 at room temperature and forms a green coloured 1 : 2 [Ni II  : 4-HBTS] complex with stability constant 2.76 × 1011 . The complex has maximum absorption at 362 nm. The molar absorptivity and Sandell’s sensitivity for the coloured solution are found to be 2.13 × 10 4  M –1  cm –1  and 0.0027   µg/cm 2  respectively. Beer’s law is obeyed in the range of 0.117–1.117 µg/mL. The interference of various diverse ions has been studied. The procedure was successfully applied for the determination of nickel in water, waste water samples and in different alloy samples.
